# Golden fixtures

Use this directory for compatibility fixtures generated from the original C++ subconverter.

Recommended layout:

- `input/`: subscription strings, config files, rulesets.
- `golden/`: expected outputs generated by C++ subconverter.
- `cases.toml`: active case matrix used by `cargo test`.
- `cases.full.toml`: default full target matrix used by CI and local `cargo test`.
- `reference.toml`: pinned v0.9.0 release, commit, portable workspace-relative executable paths, and SHA-256 values.

Case fields:

- `name`: stable test name.
- `target`: subconverter target such as `clash`, `surge`, `singbox`.
- `input`: path under this directory.
- `golden`: expected output path under this directory.
- `config`: optional config path under this directory.
- `surge_version`: optional Surge version passed as `ver`.

Regenerate fixtures from the pinned C++ v0.9.0 executable:

```powershell
.\tools\generate-golden.ps1 -Manifest cases.full.toml -InlineInput
```

The default executable path and expected SHA-256 come from `reference.toml`. Put downloaded reference releases under `work/baseline/subconverter-v0.9.0/`, or pass an explicit `-SubconverterExe`; the executable is accepted only when it matches the pinned hash. `-InlineInput` avoids an unnecessary fixture HTTP listener for direct-link fixtures.

If the C++ executable is running somewhere else, such as a VM, container, or manually started local process, point the generator at that service instead. In this mode the script does not start or stop the C++ executable:

```powershell
.\tools\generate-golden.ps1 -BaseUrl http://127.0.0.1:25500
```

When the C++ service runs in a container, expose fixture files from a host HTTP server and pass direct proxy links inline to avoid container loopback and C++ remote-subscription parsing differences:

```powershell
python -m http.server 30079 --bind 0.0.0.0 --directory tests\fixtures
.\tools\generate-golden.ps1 -BaseUrl http://127.0.0.1:30080 -NoFixtureServer -FixtureBaseUrl http://host.docker.internal:30079 -Manifest cases.full.toml -InlineInput
```

If the executable is missing, build or locate it first:

```powershell
$env:SUBCONVERTER_CPP_SOURCE = "C:\path\to\subconverter"
.\tools\build-cpp-subconverter.ps1 -PreferMsys2
```

Use `-InstallMsys2Deps` only when the local MSYS2 package database is healthy and you want the script to install native build dependencies.

Generate the full target matrix:

```powershell
.\tools\generate-golden.ps1 -SubconverterExe C:\path\to\subconverter.exe -Manifest cases.full.toml -InlineInput
```

Validate metadata and run the full semantic comparison after regeneration:

```powershell
.\tools\generate-golden.ps1 -Manifest cases.full.toml -ValidateOnly
cargo test -p subconverter-core --test golden
```
